body.question_1: How would you rate the technical competency of the student intern in their relevant field of study?
body.answer_1: The intern from the School of Business Administration's Human Resource Management program demonstrated excellent technical competency that significantly exceeded our initial expectations for a student at this stage of their academic career. Their foundational knowledge in core discipline areas was solid, well-structured, and readily applicable to real workplace challenges encountered during the internship period. They adapted quickly to our internal tools, methodologies, and technical standards, requiring minimal remedial instruction before becoming a productive and largely self-directed contributor to assigned projects. The depth of practical skills, particularly in applied problem-solving and technical documentation, was notably strong and reflects well on the quality of the School of Business Administration's curriculum design and delivery.

body.question_2: How well did the intern demonstrate professional communication and interpersonal skills in the workplace?
body.answer_2: This intern's professionalism was fair, showing genuine commitment and a positive attitude that formed a solid foundation, with some areas requiring coaching and development during the placement period. Reliability and punctuality were good overall, though proactive communication about progress and obstacles was inconsistent and required encouragement from supervisors. The intern engaged positively with team members and accepted feedback constructively in most situations, demonstrating the willingness to learn that is essential for professional growth. Additional preparation in formal workplace communication — particularly written reporting standards and stakeholder update practices — would improve the professional impact of graduates from this program.

body.question_3: How effectively did the intern collaborate with team members and contribute to group projects?
body.answer_3: The intern integrated into our team environment exceptionally and became a valued collaborative contributor from an early stage of the internship, consistently demonstrating the interpersonal skills required to function effectively in a professional team setting. They participated actively in team meetings, contributed ideas respectfully and constructively, and took clear ownership of their assigned workstreams without requiring constant supervision or direction from colleagues. During group project phases, the intern's ability to coordinate tasks, manage shared deadlines, and maintain positive relationships under pressure was exceptional and significantly contributed to successful outcomes. These teamwork capabilities reflect very well on the interpersonal skills development embedded in the School of Business Administration's Human Resource Management program.

body.question_4: How would you assess the intern's problem-solving abilities and approach to challenging or unfamiliar tasks?
body.answer_4: The intern's problem-solving abilities were among the most excellent attributes they brought to our organization from the School of Business Administration's Human Resource Management program. When confronted with unfamiliar challenges or technical obstacles, the intern approached them systematically — breaking problems into manageable components, forming and testing hypotheses, and escalating appropriately only when genuinely blocked by limitations beyond their current capability. Their intellectual curiosity, persistence under pressure, and willingness to explore unconventional solutions made them a genuinely valuable problem-solver within the team. This quality is difficult to teach in the workplace and speaks highly of the critical thinking culture embedded in the School of Business Administration's academic programs.

body.question_5: How quickly and effectively did the intern adapt to your company's work culture, tools, and environment?
body.answer_5: Adaptability to changing conditions and evolving requirements was a substandard challenge for this intern from the School of Business Administration's Human Resource Management program, and it became a recurring obstacle during the internship period. When project scope or priorities shifted — as they regularly do in a professional environment — the intern's response was often slow and required significant reassurance and directive guidance from supervisors. Tolerance for ambiguity and the ability to operate effectively without complete information or a fully defined task specification were noticeably underdeveloped relative to our expectations. We recommend the School of Business Administration expose students to more dynamic, ambiguity-tolerant project environments during their studies to develop this critical professional competency before graduation.

body.question_6: How proficient was the intern in English communication, both written and verbal, for professional purposes?
body.answer_6: English communication proficiency was a inconsistent limitation for this intern from the School of Business Administration's Human Resource Management program that impacted their effectiveness in several key areas of their role. Written communication — including emails and technical documentation — frequently contained grammatical and structural errors that required editing by supervisors before use in internal or external contexts. Verbal participation in English-language meetings was hesitant, limiting the intern's ability to contribute fully to discussions or to represent the team confidently in broader organizational settings. We strongly recommend the School of Business Administration elevate its English communication training requirements — particularly for professional writing and spoken business English — as a core graduation competency for all programs.

body.question_7: How well did the intern's academic training align with the practical demands and standards of your industry?
body.answer_7: Based on our experience with this intern from the School of Business Administration's Human Resource Management program, we are superbly impressed with the alignment between the academic curriculum and the practical demands of our industry. The foundational knowledge, theoretical frameworks, and applied skills the intern brought to the role mapped closely to what we require in graduate-level professional practitioners in this field. In particular, the project-based and problem-solving components of the curriculum appear to have translated well into workplace readiness. We would encourage the School of Business Administration to continue developing its industry connections and to involve practitioners actively in curriculum review to maintain this strong alignment as the professional landscape continues to evolve.

body.question_8: What specific technical or soft skill areas do you believe the university curriculum should strengthen for future graduates?
body.answer_8: Our curriculum-related feedback for the School of Business Administration's Human Resource Management program, based on this internship experience, is satisfactory: there is a reasonable foundation but meaningful gaps that the institution could address to significantly improve graduate readiness. Core theoretical knowledge is adequately covered, and the intern was able to apply foundational concepts with reasonable effectiveness. However, gaps exist in applied tool proficiency, project management fundamentals, and the professional communication standards expected in industry settings. We recommend more regular and structured industry consultations to ensure the curriculum remains current, and greater emphasis on workplace simulation through live projects, internships, and industry-sponsored assessments.

body.question_9: How would you rate the intern's overall performance and value contribution to your organization during the placement?
body.answer_9: The overall performance of this intern from the School of Business Administration's Human Resource Management program has been excellent and has added genuine value to our organization during the internship period in ways we did not fully anticipate at the outset. Key deliverables were completed on time, to the required quality standard, and in some cases with additional initiative that exceeded the brief. The intern's contributions to ongoing team projects have been meaningful and will have a lasting impact beyond the duration of their placement. We are very satisfied with the investment we made in hosting this intern and have recommended internally that we continue and expand our partnership with the School of Business Administration's internship program in the coming academic year.

body.question_10: Based on your experience with this intern, would you consider hiring from this university in the future, and why?
body.answer_10: Based on this intern's performance and the overall quality of graduates we have observed from the School of Business Administration's Human Resource Management program, we would absolutely consider hiring from this institution in the future — both for internships and for permanent graduate positions. The intern's technical competency, professional attitude, collaborative skills, and growth mindset align closely with the profile we seek when making graduate hiring decisions. We are actively discussing a return internship offer for this individual and have already flagged the School of Business Administration as a preferred graduate source institution for our talent acquisition team. We would encourage the School of Business Administration to continue deepening its industry partnerships and look forward to an ongoing and mutually beneficial relationship with the institution in the years ahead.
